chief_complaint: fatigue and mild nausea for 2 hours, feeling generally unwell, came on her own

hidden_diagnosis: Silent MI — diabetic women often present without chest pain. Low BP 88/60 indicates cardiogenic shock.

chief_complaint: mild shortness of breath since morning, had knee replacement surgery 5 days ago, otherwise feels okay
history: bilateral knee osteoarthritis, surgery 5 days ago, been on bed rest, no DVT prophylaxis given
hidden_diagnosis: Pulmonary Embolism — post-surgical immobility + tachycardia + hypoxia. Classic PE presentation that looks mild.

chief_complaint: just feeling confused since morning, family says he is not his usual self, mild fever
history: benign prostatic hyperplasia with indwelling catheter, diabetes, lives alone most of the week
hidden_diagnosis: Urosepsis — elderly with catheter + confusion + fever + low BP meets SIRS criteria. Sepsis often presents subtly in elderly.

chief_complaint: mild indigestion and upper stomach discomfort after dinner, some sweating, feeling anxious
history: diabetes type 2 for 15 years, hypothyroidism, peripheral neuropathy, family history of heart disease
hidden_diagnosis: Acute Coronary Syndrome — atypical presentation in diabetic woman. Diaphoresis + low BP + DM history. Classic missed MI.

chief_complaint: mild headache after minor road traffic accident, was wearing helmet, no loss of consciousness, GCS 14, feels mostly fine
history: no medical conditions, motorcycle accident at low speed, hit a divider, slight drowsiness
hidden_diagnosis: Evolving epidural hematoma — lucid interval classic presentation. GCS 14 with drowsiness post-RTA requires urgent CT. Can deteriorate rapidly.
